label: Authority Legitimacy Validation Pattern
description: TSMM-native pattern for checking whether presented authority remains legitimate at execution time.

nodes.label: Authority legitimacy validation domain
nodes.description: Domain where authority must be checked for legitimacy, not only presence.

nodes.label: Policy authority
nodes.description: Defines the policy and accountability thresholds for legitimate control.

nodes.label: Authority issuer
nodes.description: Issues delegated authority subject to policy and scope.

nodes.label: Legitimacy validation policy
nodes.description: Requires issuer trust, scope validity, revocation freshness, and contextual admissibility.

nodes.label: Legitimacy verifier
nodes.description: Evaluates whether authority is still legitimate before consequential action.

nodes.label: Legitimacy decision
nodes.description: Produces allow, deny, or escalate outcomes based on the legitimacy check.

nodes.label: Legitimacy decision receipt
nodes.description: Captures policy, issuer, scope, revocation, and context inputs used in the decision.

nodes.label: Execution boundary effect
nodes.description: Either permits execution, blocks execution, or routes to human review.

nodes.label: Relying service
nodes.description: Consumes the decision and enforces the resulting boundary.

edges.description: Policy authority governs the legitimacy policy.
Issuer confers technically enforceable authority to the agent.
Legitimacy policy constrains execution based on validity conditions.
Verifier checks legitimacy of the authority being presented.
Verifier output informs the trust decision.
Decision produces a receipt that can be audited later.
Decision determines whether execution is allowed, denied, or escalated.
The relying service enforces the execution boundary effect.
